name: Climb to the port of El Remedio — Circular from Chelva
name_original: Subida al puerto El Remedio — Circular desde Chelva
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Chelva traverse a diverse landscape characterized by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and historical features. The region offers varied elevation changes, with routes ascending to significant points like Puerto del Remedio. Cyclists can explore areas featuring ancient aqueducts and the serene River Chelva, providing a mix of challenging climbs and scenic stretches. This area is well-suited for road cycling due to its mostly paved surfaces and varied topography. Best road cycling routes around Chelva The most popular road cycling route is Climb to the port of El Remedio — Circular from Chelva, a 33.2 miles (53.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete, featuring a significant ascent to the port. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Christian Quarter of Ollerías – Andalusí Quarter of Benacacira loop from Chelva, a difficult 26.7 miles (43.0 km) path. This route winds through historical quarters, showcasing the region's cultural richness. Local road cyclists also love the El Remedio Pass Ascent – Puebla de San Miguel Village loop from Calles, a 91.0 miles (146.5 km) trail leading through varied mountainous terrain, often completed in about 7 hours 57 minutes. Road cycling around Chelva is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and historical aqueducts. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Chelva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Chelva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Chelva?
A: Chelva offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 20 routes available for exploration. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, including mountainous terrain and river valleys.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for road cycling in Chelva?
A: Road cycling routes in Chelva are generally suited for cyclists with some experience, primarily featuring moderate to difficult ability levels. You can expect significant elevation changes, with routes ascending to points like Puerto del Remedio.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for experienced road cyclists looking for a challenge?
A: Yes, experienced road cyclists will find plenty of challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31438361" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Remedio Pass Ascent – Puebla de San Miguel Village loop from Calles&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 91-mile trail with substantial elevation gain, often taking nearly 8 hours to complete. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33724434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Benagéber Reservoir – Azud de Tuéjar loop from Calles&lt;/a&gt;, which covers almost 50 miles with over 1600 meters of ascent.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while road cycling in Chelva?
A: Chelva's road cycling routes offer access to remarkable historical sites. You can encounter the ancient Roman &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1037041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Cortada Aqueduct&lt;/a&gt;, a significant engineering marvel. The town itself boasts historic Arab and Jewish quarters, and routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31433397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Christian Quarter of Ollerías – Andalusí Quarter of Benacacira loop from Chelva&lt;/a&gt; wind through these culturally rich areas.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or viewpoints along the routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in natural beauty. You can find recreational areas along the River Chelva, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1031861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Playeta de Chelva&lt;/a&gt; offers a beautiful spot by the river. Routes also provide views of the Benagéber Reservoir and ascents to points like Puerto del Remedio, offering panoramic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5137351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Ropé&lt;/a&gt; is another notable summit in the area.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Chelva?
A: Chelva is an excellent destination for road cycling throughout much of the year. The region's diverse landscapes are enjoyable in spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Even in winter, many routes remain accessible, though it's always advisable to check local weather conditions.

Q: Is there convenient parking available for road cyclists in Chelva?
A: While specific parking areas for cyclists are not detailed, Chelva is a town well-accustomed to outdoor enthusiasts. Parking is generally available within or near the town center, providing access to the start points of many routes.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming near road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1031861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Playeta de Chelva&lt;/a&gt; is a popular recreational area along the Chelva river, known for its swimming hole, making it a perfect spot to cool off after a ride. The Ruta del Agua also features waterfalls and river areas.

Q: Are there many circular road cycling routes available?
A: Many of Chelva's road cycling routes are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. An example is the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e839892735/climb-to-the-port-of-el-remedio-circular-from-chelva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the port of El Remedio — Circular from Chelva&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 33-mile loop.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Chelva?
A: The road cycling experience in Chelva is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Cyclists frequently praise the varied topography, the mix of challenging climbs and scenic stretches, and the opportunity to explore historical features like ancient aqueducts and charming villages.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or find accommodation near the routes?
A: Chelva, with its historic quarters and status as a living museum, offers various amenities. You'll find cafes and restaurants within the town, and recreational areas along the River Chelva provide picnic spots. Accommodation options are also available in and around Chelva for those planning an extended stay.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through traditional villages?
A: Yes, several routes are designed to showcase the region's cultural richness by passing through traditional villages and historical quarters. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31433397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Christian Quarter of Ollerías – Andalusí Quarter of Benacacira loop from Chelva&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, winding through ancient settlements.

Q: What kind of surfaces can I expect on the road cycling routes?
A: The road cycling routes around Chelva primarily feature mostly paved surfaces, making them ideal for road bikes. This ensures a smooth ride while still allowing you to experience the diverse and often mountainous terrain of the region.
tips: A demanding but very picturesque climb from the town of Chelva to the port of El Remedio with an altitude of 999 m above sea level. The views are very comforting and the road surface is in very good condition.

A very pretty town located in a very picturesque setting within the region of La Serranía. You can see very nice views both from the town and from the road to there. A highly recommended place to visit.
